what_it_does: A first-run operational findings log for the resident local 4B model, recording configuration drift, throughput measurements, root-caused failures, in-system tooling lessons, and action-tagged takeaways intended to guide upgrades, guides, and fixes.
observations: The live endpoint/model differs from the registered chat-4b entry (:8001 FP8 64k vs :8000 AWQ 16k).
Actual context length is 65536, not 16384, so far fewer files are oversize than expected.
max-num-seqs is 2, making Tim's target concurrency of 32 impossible without a loadout change.
Reasoning is on by default and expensive; a short answer consumed ~1037 tokens and ~12s, and can return content: None.
Quality on a trivial sample was good: it correctly flagged missing module-level constants.
Tim directed the author to use the MCP/role surface (run_draft, run_role, run_draft_items) instead of a bespoke urllib harness.
run_role exposes think=False, verified to cut token cost dramatically.
Existing roles such as interpret_file and dragnet_coarse/fine/design already cover the interpretive sweep the author had rebuilt bespoke.
A stale provider binding after loadout swap required an MCP reconnect to refresh.
The coverage audit was validated in-system on three samples using run_draft and an output_schema.
Raising concurrency caused an OOM and the model could not restart even at the known-good config due to near-full shared GPU/fragmentation.
FP8 chat-4b-fp8 was pathologically slow (~11 tok/s) compared with AWQ (~100 tok/s single-stream, ~2334 tok/s warm 32-conc); AWQ was swapped in.
Only three code files exceed the 65k-token context limit.
Cold JIT recompilation of hybrid Mamba/Gated-DeltaNet Triton kernels per input shape explains the cold-vs-warm concurrency anomaly.
A class of stale process-cached model-derived state was root-caused in _GLOBAL_VRAM_GATE and fixed by rebuilding the gate when the registry limit changes.
standouts: The FP8-vs-AWQ throughput root cause and swap is the central operational pivot.
The explicit rejection of the urllib harness in favor of existing engine roles (run_role/run_draft_items) is the main design pivot.
F-10 and F-11 define a class of bug: model-loadout-derived process state must be resolved at call-time or invalidated on swap, not frozen at first creation.
conventions: Markdown journal with dated entries and findings tagged by implied action.
Uses Tim quotes to record corrections and decisions.
Records provenance via run:// turn/role URLs.
Validates structured output through an output_schema.
Distinguishes verified results, owned mistakes, and open follow-ups.
concerns: The log mixes resolved fixes, unresolved open questions, and operational mistakes in one narrative, which may be hard to scan for action status.
Some actions depend on external loadout decisions (Tim / other session) and are not self-contained.
The FP8 pathological behavior is noted as not fully proven and left for later debugging.
notes: A raw, action-tagged diary of getting the local 4B to work at scale, culminating in a swap to AWQ and a decision to rebuild the audit as an in-system role.
questions: What is the exact schema of the registry/services entry that should be updated for the live endpoint?
What are the full specifications of the existing interpret_file, dragnet_coarse, dragnet_fine, and dragnet_design roles?
Who or what is 'Tim' in this context, and what authority does that voice represent?
What is the precise content of the coverage_audit role or draft spec used for the validated runs?
How is SlotBudget.from_registry() implemented, and what other cached singletons might still be loadout-sensitive?
What is the 'company config' CLI and how does it interact with the VRAM manager?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is explicitly framed as first-run findings for upgrades/guides/fixes, and it delivers exactly that.
apparent_themes: Resolve loadout-derived state at call-time or invalidate it on swap
Prefer in-system roles and provenance over bespoke scripts
Treat GPU/VRAM concurrency as a managed loadout resource, not a tunable knob
Root-cause operational anomalies rather than accepting symptom-level explanations
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn a messy first hands-on session with a local 4B into durable, evidence-based guidance that reshapes the coverage/interpretive audit to use existing engine machinery and prevents future loadout/state staleness bugs.
novelty: It reads as an unvarnished operational post-mortem that includes owned mistakes (OOMing the brain, stale singleton gate), JIT-kernel root-cause speculation, a concrete FP8/AWQ throughput swap, and an explicit pivot away from a standalone urllib harness toward the Company's existing role engine.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, action-tagged record of actual model behavior under specific loadouts, plus the derived principle that model-loadout-derived process state must track the live registry; this helps future tooling avoid stale caches and choose the right in-system invocation path.
summary_for_embedding: A dated operational findings log documenting the first use of a resident local 4B model, including endpoint drift, throughput benchmarking, an FP8-to-AWQ swap, coverage audit validation, and root-caused lessons about stale process-cached model state.
intention_for_embedding: To capture first-run lessons and actions so the coverage/interpretive audit can be rebuilt using the Company's existing role engine and so loadout/concurrency decisions are grounded in measured behavior.
